# Nytheria Game Project Intake Fixture

TSF-local mock/evidence fixture for Game Forge V1. Evidence only; not approved canon.

## Project Identity

- Game name: Nytheria
- Old names / aliases: Nytheris
- Genre: living fantasy world RPG / AI-assisted world simulator
- Camera/view style: text-first simulation cockpit, later map and scene views
- Single-player/co-op/multiplayer: single-player first
- Target platform: local desktop/web prototype first

## Player Promise

- Core fantasy / player promise: enter a high-magic, low-tech world that moves without you; you are not automatically the chosen one.
- World structure: regions, factions, gods, wars, rumors, default timelines, and local histories.
- Combat style: undecided; prototype should avoid real combat until world state and consequence loops work.
- Progression style: race, class, origin, faith, relationships, reputation, faction standing, and story consequences.

## Simulation And AI

- AI/living-world needs: factions pursue goals, gods respond to faith, wars progress, rumors mutate, NPCs remember, and timeline events fire without the player.
- Lore/canon constraints: old lore/root files are backbone evidence and must not be freely rewritten; distinguish approved canon from adaptation proposals.
- Save/load expectations: world state snapshots, player action ledger, timeline checkpoint, and deterministic fixture saves.
- Modding/content pipeline needs: lore registry, canon tiers, faction data, region state, event definitions, rumor seeds, NPC memory records.

## Prototype Boundary

- Prototype goal: text-only world clock plus faction and rumor toy simulations.
- Off-limits: full game engine, full content database, real-time AI game master, procedural infinite lore, multiplayer, graphics pipeline, combat engine.
- Must-ask-Tim decisions: canon tier rules, Nytheris-to-Nytheria source truth, starting region, playable origins, faith/god boundaries, default human doom timeline.

## Evidence Boundary

- Nytheris remains an alias/backbone context, not discarded.
- Lore is evidence until Tim marks it approved canon.
- Adaptation proposals must be labeled and cannot overwrite root lore by default.
